chunks.text: Ana C. Arias Elected to the National Academy of Inventors as 2025 Fellow | Berkeley Wireless Research Center Skip to main content Ana C. Arias Elected to the National Academy of Inventors as 2025 Fellow December 16, 2025 UC Berkeley professors Ana Cl

ow December 16, 2025 UC Berkeley professors Ana Claudia Arias and Tahir Ghani have been elected as 2025 Fellows of the National Academy of Inventors (NAI), one of the highest professional distinctions awarded to academic inventors. BWRC is proud to h

awarded to academic inventors. BWRC is proud to highlight Professor Ana Claudia Arias, a longtime BWRC faculty member, in recognition of her outstanding contributions to flexible electronics, printed sensors, and wearable health technologies. Electi

sensors, and wearable health technologies. Election to NAI Fellow honors inventors whose work has made a significant impact on quality of life, economic development, and the welfare of society through innovation and commercialization. Professor Aria

h innovation and commercialization. Professor Arias’s research bridges materials science, electrical engineering, and bioelectronics, and her innovations have led to numerous patents, startups, and real-world applications. Her election reflects both

al-world applications. Her election reflects both the global impact of her work and BWRC’s continued leadership in advancing transformative technologies. The National Academy of Inventors includes fellows from leading universities, research instituti

lows from leading universities, research institutions, and companies worldwide. Election as a Fellow is a prestigious recognition of sustained innovation and inventorship. Congratulations to Professor Ana Claudia Arias on this well-deserved honor. ht
Ana Claudia Arias on this well-deserved honor. https://engineering.berkeley.edu/news/2025/12/two-uc-berkeley-professors-elected…
